package.name: cs603_particle_filter
package.description:
COMPSCI-603 Project 3: Robot Localization Using Particle Filters.
This package implements a particle filter for robot localization in a simulated house environment using a Triton robot equipped with a 2D LiDAR.
This package is based on the starter code from: https://gitlab.com/HCRLab/stingray-robotics/cs603_particle_filter
=== Deliverable 1: Motion and Sensor Models ===
The package includes: - An odometry-based motion model (scripts/motion_model.py) - A likelihood field sensor model (scripts/sensor_model.py) - A precomputed likelihood field / distance matrix (maps/likelihood_field.npy) - A metric map of the house environment (maps/metric_map.png)
=== How to Run ===
Step 1: Build the package cd ~/catkin_ws catkin_make source devel/setup.bash
Step 2: Launch the simulation (Gazebo + RViz + particle filter node) roslaunch cs603_particle_filter particle_filter.launch
Step 3: Drive the robot using teleop (in a separate terminal) rosrun cs603_particle_filter teleop_particle_filter.py
The launch file will automatically: - Spawn the Triton robot in the house environment in Gazebo - Serve the saved map via map_server - Launch RViz for visualization - Run the particle_filter.py node which executes the motion model and sensor model
In RViz, add the following displays to visualize: - Map (topic: /map) to see the occupancy grid - LaserScan (topic: /scan) to see the LiDAR readings - Odometry (topic: /odom) to see the robot pose
=== File Descriptions ===
scripts/particle_filter.py - Main ROS node (launched automatically) scripts/motion_model.py - Odometry-based motion model module scripts/sensor_model.py - Likelihood field sensor model module scripts/generate_likelihood_field.py - Offline script to compute the likelihood field scripts/teleop_particle_filter.py - Keyboard teleop for robot control (provided) scripts/position_publisher.py - TF helper node (provided by starter package) maps/map.yaml - Map metadata maps/map.pgm - Occupancy grid map image maps/likelihood_field.npy - Precomputed distance matrix (used at runtime) maps/likelihood_field.png - Likelihood field visualization (for submission) maps/metric_map.png - Metric map visualization (for submission)
=== Starter Package ===
This project uses the starter package provided by HCRLab at: https://gitlab.com/HCRLab/stingray-robotics/cs603_particle_filter The motion model, sensor model, likelihood field generation, and particle filter code were implemented from scratch by the student.
package.author: Khushei Meda
